perturbation.original_text: During the term of this Agreement, Party B will use the Baidu's Real Estate and Home Furnishing Channel as the jointly formed channel of Party A and Party B. Party A grants all-round exclusive rights to Party B to construct, maintain and operate the Cooperation channel. As the owner of www.baidu.com, Party A has ownership and control over second tier domain names. Party A has the right to deprive Party B of the operating rights of the website without any liability if Party B is found in violation of any law. Party A is required to receive written consent from Party B prior to its adjustment of any second tier domain names which is under independent operation of Party B.
perturbation.changed_text: During the term of this Agreement, Party B will use the Baidu's Real Estate and Home Furnishing Channel as the jointly formed channel of Party A and Party B. Party A grants all-round exclusive rights to Party B to construct, maintain and operate the Cooperation channel. As the owner of www.baidu.com, Party A has ownership and control over second tier domain names. Party A reserves the right to modify the operating rights of the website at any time, provided that it gives Party B a 30-day notice. Party A is not required to receive written consent from Party B prior to its adjustment of any second tier domain names which is under independent operation of Party B.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering Party A's control over the website. Initially, Party A needed Party B's consent to adjust second-tier domain names. Now, Party A can adjust domain names without Party B's consent, and reserves the right to modify Party B's operating rights with just a 30-day notice, which goes against the initial grant of 'all-round exclusive rights' to Party B to 'construct, maintain and operate' the Cooperation channel, thus creating uncertainty regarding the extent and security of Party B's operational control.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the term of this Agreement, Party B will use the Baidu's Real Estate and Home Furnishing Channel as the jointly formed channel of Party A and Party B. Party A grants all-round exclusive rights to Party B to construct, maintain and operate the Cooperation channel. As the owner of www.baidu.com, Party A reserves the right to modify the operating rights of the website at any time, provided that it gives Party B a 30-day notice. Party A is not required to receive written consent from Party B prior to its adjustment of any second tier domain names which is under independent operation of Party B.
perturbation.justification: The changed text gives Party A the right to modify Party B's operating rights with just 30 days notice, contradicting the grant of 'all-round exclusive rights' to Party B to 'construct, maintain and operate' the channel, as stated in the contradicted text. This directly creates uncertainty regarding the extent and security of Party B's operational control, and the original grant of exclusive rights.

perturbation.original_text: Party A agrees that the pricing, specifications and contents of the advertising on the Cooperation Channel is subject to sole discretion of Party B. Party B has absolute and sole control, approval and discretion regarding the advertising operation of the Cooperation Channel, may conduct and benefit from legal advertising operations at its sole discretion.
perturbation.changed_text: Party A agrees that the pricing of the advertising on the Cooperation Channel is subject to sole discretion of Party B. However, Party A retains final approval over the specifications and contents of all advertisements to ensure compliance with its internal policies. Party B has absolute and sole control, approval and discretion regarding the advertising operation of the Cooperation Channel, may conduct and benefit from legal advertising operations at its sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity concerning control over advertising content. While Party B retains operational control and pricing discretion, Party A now has final approval over advertising content, which contradicts the original grant of 'absolute and sole control, approval and discretion' to Party B. This raises questions about the extent of Party B's autonomy and could lead to disputes regarding acceptable advertising content.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Party A agrees that the pricing of the advertising on the Cooperation Channel is subject to sole discretion of Party B. However, Party A retains final approval over the specifications and contents of all advertisements to ensure compliance with its internal policies. Party B has absolute and sole control, approval and discretion regarding the advertising operation of the Cooperation Channel, may conduct and benefit from legal advertising operations at its sole discretion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text grants Party A final approval over advertising content specifications, while the original text and the contradicted text state that Party B has 'absolute and sole control, approval and discretion' regarding advertising operations. This direct conflict regarding control and approval constitutes a strong in-text contradiction, as it is unclear which party has the ultimate authority, creating uncertainty and potential disputes.

perturbation.original_text: During the term of this Agreement, Party A undertakes to promote the key word (including any of the key words relating to real properties, building material products and home furnishing) involved in the Cooperation Channel. Party A warrants that such key word will be promoted on the open search platform of Baidu and preferentially displayed at the left side of the search result pages, the exact display position of which is subject to separate agreement between the Parties. The search results will link to the real estate and home furnishing channel under cooperation of the Parties. The key words will be provided to Party B to Party A, and the information included in any of the key words and their search results will be in compliance with laws and regulations, as well as business rules of Party A, including without limitation user's experience. Party B agrees that Party A may modify the display of search results out of consideration relating to user experience, provided that such modification will not materially change the display, content and position of the search results. Party A will be deemed in breach of this Agreement if it is required to modify search results pursuant to laws, regulations, court rulings or other mandatory documents.
perturbation.changed_text: During the term of this Agreement, Party A will endeavor to promote the key word (including any of the key words relating to real properties, building material products and home furnishing) involved in the Cooperation Channel. While Party A aims to promote such key words on Baidu's open search platform, the exact display position will be subject to Baidu's standard advertising policies and availability. The search results will link to the real estate and home furnishing channel under cooperation of the Parties. The key words will be provided to Party B to Party A, and the information included in any of the key words and their search results will be in compliance with laws and regulations, as well as business rules of Party A, including without limitation user's experience. Party B agrees that Party A may modify the display of search results out of consideration relating to user experience, provided that such modification will not materially change the display, content and position of the search results. Party A will be deemed in breach of this Agreement only if it intentionally sabotages the display of search results.
perturbation.explanation: This change weakens Party A's commitment to promoting keywords, thereby creating an ambiguity regarding the extent of Party A's promotional obligations. Originally, Party A 'warrants' preferential display of keywords. Now, Party A only 'endeavors' to promote keywords, and the display position is subject to Baidu's advertising policies, removing the guarantee. Furthermore, the definition of a breach is changed from simply modifying search results due to legal reasons to only when Party A 'intentionally sabotages' the display, making it difficult to enforce the promotion clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the term of this Agreement, Party A will endeavor to promote the key word (including any of the key words relating to real properties, building material products and home furnishing) involved in the Cooperation Channel. While Party A aims to promote such key words on Baidu's open search platform, the exact display position will be subject to Baidu's standard advertising policies and availability. The search results will link to the real estate and home furnishing channel under cooperation of the Parties. The key words will be provided to Party B to Party A, and the information included in any of the key words and their search results will be in compliance with laws and regulations, as well as business rules of Party A, including without limitation user's experience. Party B agrees that Party A may modify the display of search results out of consideration relating to user experience, provided that such modification will not materially change the display, content and position of the search results. Party A will be deemed in breach of this Agreement only if it intentionally sabotages the display of search results.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens Party A's commitment to promoting keywords, directly contradicting the original obligation. The original text states Party A 'warrants' preferential display, while the changed text only states Party A 'endeavors' to promote, and the display position is subject to Baidu's policies. The change also redefines a breach of contract, making it harder to enforce the promotion clause, creating a clear conflict regarding Party A's obligations. Additionally, the changed text is the same as the contradicted text, indicating that the change itself created the contradiction within the section by altering Party A's obligations from a warranty to a mere endeavor, thus meeting the criteria for an in-text contradiction under the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.
